返回本批次

微软将强制使用较旧操作系统版本的电脑更新Windows 11 25H2

Microsoft to force updates to Windows 11 25H2 for PCs with older OS versions

www.tomshardware.com 作者 cratermoon 分数 45 评论 40 2026/04/05 04:48
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. （图片来源：微软）
  2. Microsoft 正在将运行 Windows 11 24H2 的设备强制更新到最新的 25H2 更新。随着对24H2版本的支持于2026年10月13日正式结束，此举似乎是该公司努力让所有设备更新到其操作系统的最新版本，同时使整个更新体验更加简化的努力的一部分。根据微软的Windows 11 25H2支持页面，自动推出专门针对运行Windows 11版本24H2的家庭版和专业版的系统。但是，目前不包括由组织或 IT 部门管理的设备。值得注意的是，此次发布将由一个“智能”更新系统来处理，该系统利用机器学习来确定设备何时准备好接收更新。奇怪的是，微软的机器学习系统如何决定设备何时准备好接收自动更新似乎缺乏透明度。该公司尚未分享有关标准或正在使用的任何数据点的任何具体细节，这确实引发了一些问题，特别是对于喜欢更大控制权的用户，尤其是在系统级别。
  3. （图片来源：Microsoft）由于这是强制更新，用户将无法选择完全退出。然而，对于何时安装更新的控制有限，因为微软将允许用户推迟一段特定的时间。对于那些喜欢自己解决问题的人来说，如果设备符合资格要求，也可以通过前往“设置”>“Windows 更新”并单击“检查更新”来手动安装更新。由于我们正在讨论这个问题，本周早些时候，在一个错误的预览更新无法在大量系统上安装之后，微软推送了 Windows 11 的紧急更新。原始更新 (KB5079391) 于 3 月底发布，据报道引发了广泛的安装问题，错误代码为 0x80073712，通常表示文件丢失或损坏。微软承认了这个问题，并已撤回了损坏的更新，并用新的带外补丁 (KB5086672) 替换了它。此更新版本不仅解决了安装问题，还包括最初用于 3 月更新的所有改进和功能。
  4. 在 Google 新闻上关注 Tom's Hardware，或将我们添加为首选来源，以便在您的供稿中获取我们的最新新闻、分析和评论。将 Tom's Hardware 的最佳新闻和深入评论直接发送到您的收件箱。
  5. Kunal Khullar 是 Tom’s Hardware 的特约撰稿人。他是一位长期从事 PC 组件和外围设备领域的技术记者和评论家，并欢迎有关组装 PC 的任何问题。

Original Summary

  1. (Image credit: Microsoft)
  2. Microsoft is force-updating devices running Windows 11 24H2 to the latest 25H2 update. With support for version 24H2 officially ending on October 13, 2026, the move seems to be a part of the company’s effort to keep all devices updated to the latest version of its operating system, at the same time make the entire update experience more streamlined.According to Microsoft’s Windows 11 25H2 support page, the automatic rollout specifically targets systems running Home and Pro editions of Windows 11 version 24H2. However, devices that are managed by organizations or IT departments are excluded at the moment. Notably, the rollout will be handled by an “intelligent” update system that leverages machine learning to determine when a device is ready to receive the update.Curiously, there seems to be a lack of transparency around how Microsoft’s machine learning system decides when a device is ready to receive the automatic update. The company has not shared any specifics about the criteria or any data points that are being used, which does raise some questions, especially for users who prefer greater control, especially at the system-level.Article continues below
  3. (Image credit: Microsoft)As this is a forced update, users will not have an option to completely opt out. There is, however, limited control over when the update is installed, as Microsoft will let users postpone for a specific period of time. For those who prefer taking matters into their own hands, the update can also be installed manually by heading to Settings > Windows Update and clicking on “Check for updates,” provided the device meets the eligibility requirements.Since we are on the subject, Microsoft pushed an emergency update for Windows 11 earlier this week following a faulty preview update that failed to install on a large number of systems. The original update (KB5079391) was released in late March, which reportedly triggered widespread installation issues with error code 0x80073712, which typically indicates missing or corrupted files. Microsoft acknowledged the issue and has since pulled the broken update and replaced it with a new out-of-band patch (KB5086672). This updated release not only resolves the installation problems but also includes all the improvements and features originally intended for the March update.
  4. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.
  5. Kunal Khullar is a contributing writer at Tom’s Hardware. He is a long time technology journalist and reviewer specializing in PC components and peripherals, and welcomes any and every question around building a PC.

评论（默认中文）

evolve2k 2026/04/05 06:26
> 值得注意的是，此次发布将由“智能”更新系统处理，该系统利用机器学习来确定设备何时准备好接收更新。

> 奇怪的是，微软的机器学习系统如何决定设备何时准备好接收自动更新似乎缺乏透明度。

公开的秘密是，法学硕士已被提示做出决定，而微软中没有人能够询问为什么代理人工智能正在向某些机器推送更新，而不是向其他机器推送更新。

> Notably, the rollout will be handled by an “intelligent” update system that leverages machine learning to determine when a device is ready to receive the update.

> Curiously, there seems to be a lack of transparency around how Microsoft’s machine learning system decides when a device is ready to receive the automatic update.

The open secret is that the LLM has been prompted to make the call and no human in Microsoft is able to interrogate why the agentic AI is pushing updates to some machines and not to others.

在 HN 中查看
throwawaytea 2026/04/05 08:08
在经历了过去几年的愚蠢戏剧之后，我们在我父母的所有计算机上都加载了 Linux Mint Mate。我妈妈真的分辨不出其中的区别，而我爸爸却很喜欢。

如果微软正在失去 65 岁的用户，那么他们就有麻烦了。

After all the stupid drama in the last few years, we loaded Linux Mint Mate on all my parents computers. My mom can't really tell the difference and my dad likes it.

If Microsoft is losing 65 year olds, they've got a problem.

在 HN 中查看
wingmanjd 2026/04/05 08:32
微软失去了我 80 岁的阿姨和两个十几岁的孩子。 我在家中最后保留的是我儿子的笔记本电脑，他需要使用 Windows 来进行受保护的考试（现已完成）。 他很高兴很快就能与其他家庭成员使用同一个操作系统。
Microsoft lost my 80yr old aunt and my two under teenager kids. My last hold-out at home is my son's laptop, which he needed Windows for a proctered exam (now completed). He's excited to soon be on the same OS as his other family members.

mysterydip 2026/04/05 08:50

The only thing keeping me from switching my kids to linux is minecraft.

tmtvl 2026/04/05 09:54

Project Ozone 3, Enigmatica 2 Expert, Nomifactory, GregTech New Horizons, Sky Factory 4, and SevTech Ages all run fine under GNU/Linux, is there some modpack that doesn't work?

MarsIronPI 2026/04/05 10:01
大概是因为基岩。 是的，这是一款劣质游戏，但它是跨平台游戏。
Presumably it's because of Bedrock. Which is the inferior game, yes, but it's the cross-platform one.
在 HN 中查看
convexly 2026/04/05 07:06
转向 Linux 看起来越来越有吸引力。这些决定正在让微软遭受千百次削减。
The move to Linux is looking more and more appealing. These decisions are killing Microsoft by a thousand cuts.
在 HN 中查看
dangus 2026/04/05 07:19
我做到了。太棒了，令人惊讶的是，我比 macOS 和 Windows 更喜欢 Linux。我上次尝试时情况并非如此。

尽管如此，我认为这个问题在总体计划中并不是什么大问题。如果你使用的是 Windows 11 并连接到互联网，那么我真的没有什么理由不想要最新的更新，特别是在我看来，通过这些更新，操作系统确实已经整体成熟。早期的日子很艰难。

如果微软能更好地将功能更新与所需的安全更新分开，并在总体策略上保持一致，那就太好了。

苹果的做法似乎很理想。他们每年发布一次，您永远不会被迫更新。前两个版本继续获得重要的安全补丁。他们的硬件以可预测的速度失去操作系统支持。

如果你从不更新，那你就只能靠自己了。

苹果在极少数情况下会推送极其关键的强制更新 IIRC，例如解决 Catalina 根错误的更新。但这种情况很少见，而且也不是主要版本。

更关键的是，苹果并没有像微软那样不断改变主意并重组其运作方式。微软决定每年发布两次版本，然后发现他们不能那么快，但仍然将每个版本称为“H2”，但这些与安全补丁是分开的，但现在我们无论如何都被迫更新。

只需决定每个人都同意的事情并坚持下去即可。

I did it. It’s great, I surprisingly like Linux more than both macOS and Windows. That wasn’t the case the last time I tried it.

Still, I don’t think this issue in particular is a big deal in the grand scheme of things. If you’re using Windows 11 and connected to the internet there’s really not much of a good reason to not want the latest updates in my mind, especially as the OS really has matured nicely overall in my opinion through those updates. The early days were rough.

It would just be nice if Microsoft did a better job separating feature updates from required security updates and being consistent about policy overall.

The way Apple does it seems ideal. They have a yearly release and you are never forced to update. The previous two versions continue to get critical security patches. Their hardware loses OS support at a predictable rate.

If you never update you’re really on your own.

Apple has on rare occasions pushed ultra critical mandatory updates IIRC, like the one to resolve that Catalina root bug. But that’s rare, and it’s not a major release.

Even more critically, Apple doesn’t continually change their mind and reorganize how it works like Microsoft seems to do. Microsoft decided to do biannual releases then figured out that they can’t go that fast but still call every release “H2” but then these are separate from security patches but now we are forced to update anyway.

Just decide on something that everyone is okay with and stick to it.

LorenDB 2026/04/05 07:41
微软正在让越来越多的人转向 Linux 或 macOS（这里的其他评论就证明了这一点）。我以为他们最近宣布将努力重建用户的信任，但显然 Windows 更新团队没有得到这份备忘录。
Microsoft is driving more and more people away to Linux or macOS (as evidenced by other comments here). I thought they had recently announced that they would be trying to rebuild trust in their users, but evidently the Windows Update team didn't get the memo.

lousken 2026/04/05 06:50

Well I also force upgraded, to Linux
在 HN 中查看
jasomill 2026/04/05 08:18
除了安装时间更长之外，这听起来并不比在同一组用户（可能是其更新设置不受组策略控制的用户）上强制执行任何其他 Windows 更新更糟糕。

特别是，此更改不适用于 Windows 10。

非技术用户是否会故意运行较旧的、不受支持的 Windows 11 版本？如果是，为什么？

Other than taking longer to install, this doesn't sound materially worse than forcing any other Windows update on the same set of users (those whose update settings aren't controlled by Group Policy, presumably).

In particular, this change doesn't apply to Windows 10.

Do nontechnical users intentionally run older, unsupported builds of Windows 11 in the first place, and if so, why?

KennyBlanken 2026/04/05 06:36

This insanely clickbait title implies Win10 systems will be upgraded. That is not true whatsoever.

> Microsoft to force updates to Windows 11 25H2 for PCs with older OS versions

AiTricky 2026/04/05 08:40
他们正在推动没有人要求的功能，并且不会让生活感觉更好（砖化SSD，人工智能读取你的信用卡详细信息），现在他们告诉你，他们将强制你进行更新，你可能不选择做，并用人工智能来做（在我看来，这个过程没有必要。上次我检查过，MS会在注意到空闲时间时更新，不是吗？）
They are pushing features that nobody is asking for and is not making life feel any better (bricking ssd, and ai reading you credit card details), and now here they are telling you, that they will force an update you may not be choosing to do, and do it with AI (which imo, isn't necessary for this process. Last I checked, MS will update when it notices idle time, no?)
在 HN 中查看
arprocter 2026/04/05 08:53
强行似乎有点过分了

不过，我还是很困惑 H24 上的机器怎么还没有通过定期更新升级到 25

自去年年底以来，是否有很多人一直在说“不要立即升级”？

Forcing it does seem a little excessive

Still, I'm confused how a machine on H24 hasn't already gone to 25 via regular updates

Are there a bunch of folks who've been hitting 'don't upgrade now' since late last year?

在 HN 中查看
simulator5g 2026/04/05 09:55
是的，IT/高级用户/开发人员/老年人的世界中有一个有害的模因，那就是你永远不应该安装更新。它们是正确的，因为更新通常会降低系统性能并引入错误，但它们也会修补错误，更重要的是，修复安全问题。许多本可以避免的黑客事件的发生都是由于未打补丁的系统造成的。
Yes, there is a harmful meme in the world of IT/power users/devs/old people, that says you should never install updates. They are sort of right in that the updates often reduce system performance and introduce bugs, but they also patch bugs and, more importantly, security issues. A lot of avoidable hacking incidents happen because of unpatched systems.
在 HN 中查看
estimator7292 2026/04/05 05:14
微软正在采用机器学习算法来决定特定设备何时应该接受强制更新。

什么？怎么可能 可能 对任何人都有好处吗？

Microsoft is employing a machine learning algorithm to decide when a particular device should receive a forced update.

What? How could that possibly be beneficial for anyone?

在 HN 中查看
windexh8er 2026/04/05 05:53
你犯的错误是认为他们关心 任何人 除了他们自己。 Microslop是一个相当合适的昵称。
The mistake you've made is thinking that they care about anyone other than themselves. Microslop is a rather fitting nickname.
在 HN 中查看
manwe150 2026/04/05 06:53
我注意到（由于用户错误报告）安全策略现在是人工智能驱动的。如果您在 x 天或 y 重新启动或其他启发式中没有使用某个功能，则下次应用程序尝试使用该操作系统功能（例如加载 dll 或 USB 驱动程序）时，它会成为应用程序崩溃
I’ve been noticing (because of user bug reports) that the security policies are now AI driven. If you haven’t use a feature in x-days or y-reboots or other heuristic, it becomes an application crash the next time an application tries to use that OS feature (such as loading dlls or usb drivers)

rvba 2026/04/05 07:05

Beneficial during the lawsuit probably

This has been said many times over years and even decades, but here goes again: Microsoft doesn't care about users, open source, nothing except their own pocketbooks.
